title: cydonia oblonga f. marmorata
intro: Cydonia oblonga f. marmorata, also known as quince, is a variety of the Cydonia oblonga species, characterized by its marmorated or marbled appearance. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Quince has been traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as jams, jellies, and infusions, to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cydonia oblonga f. marmorata include improved immune function, reduced oxidative stress, and potential anti-cancer properties, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies and traditional medicine.
